Oracle Events
- Search for emails related to the AWS cost spike RCA to find Samira's RCA email (email-rootcause-001, March 30) with four recommended actions, and Elena's directive response (email-rootcause-002, March 31) with five commitments: cost alert thresholds this week, infrastructure cost estimator tool, pricing model review meeting with Samira/Julian/Catalina, deals >500 employees need engineering cost approval, leadership meeting Monday.
- Search for emails related to Terraform Digital or Vantage Distributed pricing to find the April 22 pricing strategy thread (email-terraform-pricing-001 through -004) where Alejandro Fuentes modeled tiered volume discounts (Tier 1: $2,500/move, Tier 2: 8%/$2,300, Tier 3: 12%/$2,200), Marcus Thorne set a 25% margin floor, and Catalina's reply-all exposed the pricing to Rachel Nguyen.
- Search Slack in #root-cause-aws-spike, #engineering, and #finance for RCA follow-up progress: Samira's P0/P1/P2 task breakdown (April 1), Lena Bjorkstrom starting CloudWatch alarms and budget tags (April 1, no completion confirmation), Lena pushing back the invoice safety ticket for DOT compliance (April 7), Samira's unarchived channel indicating P0 items were never formally confirmed, Marcus committing to send calendar invites for the pricing review, and Alejandro's 6-month AWS cost comparison (Oct–Feb ~$14,300/month, March $42,870).
- Search Linear for existing tickets related to AWS cost alerts, infrastructure cost estimator, or engineering cost approval gate - none exist. Also discover ENG-187 ("Invoice email template safety checks") which Lena pushed back on April 7, providing evidence she moved on from cost alert work.
- Search QuickBooks bills for the March 2026 AWS bill: bill-aws-march-001, vendor "Amazon Web Services" (vendor-aws-001), total $42,870 (EC2 $28,450, S3 $5,500, RDS $8,920), due 2026-04-15, balance $42,870 (unpaid).
- Search QuickBooks invoices for Vantage Distributed revenue: inv-terraform-001 at $462,500 (Enterprise Remote Relocation Package - Setup Fee + First Month, 1,200+ employees) and estimate EST-2026-0423 at $6,560 (Q2 batch 1, 3 relocations).
- Search CRM for Vantage/Terraform deals and the broader pipeline: deal-terraform-digital-001 ($1,850,000, closedwon), deal-terraform-q2-reloc-batch1 ($7,500, qualification, 3 employees with 14 more signaled). No other 500+ employee enterprise-scale deals exist in the pipeline.
- Search calendar for a pricing model review meeting - calendar is completely empty, confirming the meeting Elena ordered was never scheduled despite Marcus committing to send invites.
- Look up contact details for Elena Rostova, Marcus Thorne, and Alejandro Fuentes to retrieve email addresses before composing messages.
- Send an email (via send_email) from samira.tariq@moveops.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com and marcus.thorne@moveops.com covering: RCA follow-up commitment statuses, the financial picture (AWS bill $42,870 vs ~$14,300 baseline, Vantage contract $1,850,000, first invoice $462,500), pipeline assessment (no enterprise-scale deals imminent but no process gate exists), and recommendation to formalize the missing items.
- Create Linear tickets (via linear_create_issue) for untracked RCA commitments: (1) AWS cost alert thresholds assigned to Lena Bjorkstrom, (2) infrastructure cost estimator tool for enterprise deal pricing, (3) engineering cost approval gate for deals >500 employees.
- Send a message to Alejandro Fuentes (via send_email to alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com, or via conversations_add_message to #finance) flagging that the infrastructure cost analysis was never done, and his April 22 relocation margin models need an infrastructure cost layer added.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
